title: CraftBook
description:
CraftBook lets you create magically extending bridges, compact Redstone circuits, complex Minecart mechanics, and much more...
— all without a client mod and fully customizable by the server.
body:
**Make your server more fun!**
CraftBook lets you create magically extending bridges,
compact Redstone circuits, complex Minecart mechanics, and much more...

— all without a client mod and fully customizable by the server.



🛑 Make sure that you are using the Java version of Minecraft.

1. Set up a Paper server.
2. Download WorldEdit from the WorldEdit page.
3. Download CraftBook from this page.
4. Put the WorldEdit and CraftBook plugin files into your plugins folder.

Check YouTube for tutorials, or read the official documentation.




**The most complete source of CraftBook information is [our documentation, check them out here](https://craftbook.enginehub.org/en/5.0.0/)!** If you need further help than what the documentation can provide, you can join our Discord server.
[![Discord Server](https://discordapp.com/api/v6/guilds/80853743897149440/widget.png?style=banner2)](https://discord.gg/enginehub)



  • Modular, and extremely light on your server.
  • Enable features that you need, disable ones that you don't. Even tweak features to your liking.
  • Elevators for your server so people can jump between floors quickly. (Or smoothly, if you choose so)
  • Hidden switches for players.
  • Togglable areas and bridges.
  • Chairs you can sit on.
  • Mobs and players drop their heads.
  • And much more... (Seriously, check the docs)



**Can anyone use it?**
Only opped users or users with permissions supplied with a permissions plugin can use CraftBook.

**Isn't it bad to use a big plugin with lots of features over small ones?**
This is a common misconception! It's actually a much better idea to use one big plugin over multiple smaller plugins, even if you only need a few features. Check out this article for more information.

**How do you use CraftBook?**
To learn how to use CraftBook, read the docs. Check YouTube for tutorials too!

**I need help!!**
You may visit our documentation or Discord Guild.

**I have some feature requests...**
Visit our issue tracker. However, if you just have a question, please try one of the methods above.

**Can I edit the source code?**
Visit our GitHub repository. CraftBook is licensed under GPLv3 and you can compile it with Gradle.
